events.text: Patient is a 62-year-old male of White/Caucasian ethnicity, non-Hispanic, with a BMI of 28.3 and a history of type 2 diabetes and hypertension.
At age 61, patient presented with rectal bleeding and change in bowel habits; colonoscopy revealed a near-obstructing mass in the sigmoid colon (C18.7).
Colonoscopic biopsy of sigmoid mass showed moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma (histology 8140/3), with tumor infiltrating through the muscularis propria.

Next-generation sequencing of tumor tissue identified KRAS G12D mutation (c.35G>A), BRAF wild-type, PIK3CA wild-type, microsatellite stable (MSS) by IHC with intact MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, and PMS2 expression.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at age 61 demonstrated a sigmoid colon mass with multiple bilobar hepatic metastases (largest 4.2 cm in segment VII) and no pulmonary metastases; staging T3N2M1a.

At age 61, tumor board recommended palliative systemic therapy with FOLFOX plus bevacizumab given left-sided primary, KRAS mutant status, and liver-limited metastatic disease with potential for conversion to resectability.
Baseline CEA was 142 ng/mL and CA 19-9 was 87 U/mL at time of diagnosis.
At age 61, patient initiated first-line FOLFOX (5-FU 400 mg/m2 bolus, 2400 mg/m2 over 46 hours, leucovorin 400 mg/m2, oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2) plus bevacizumab 5 mg/kg every 2 weeks.
After cycle 4 of FOLFOX, patient developed grade 2 peripheral sensory neuropathy in bilateral hands and feet, managed with dose reduction of oxaliplatin to 65 mg/m2.
At age 61, patient experienced grade 2 hypertension attributed to bevacizumab, controlled with amlodipine 5 mg daily.
Restaging CT after 6 cycles of FOLFOX-bevacizumab showed partial response with reduction in hepatic metastases (largest now 2.1 cm) and stable sigmoid primary; CEA decreased to 34 ng/mL.
At age 62, multidisciplinary tumor board reconsidered hepatic resection given excellent response to chemotherapy with now potentially resectable liver-limited disease.
At age 62, patient underwent laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy with en bloc mesenteric lymph node dissection and simultaneous right hepatectomy with wedge resection of segment II lesion.

Surgical pathology of sigmoid colectomy specimen showed residual moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma, ypT3N1b (3 of 22 lymph nodes positive), with treatment effect (40% viable tumor); hepatic resection margins were negative (R0).
At age 62, postoperative adjuvant FOLFOX (without bevacizumab) was administered for 4 additional cycles to complete a perioperative total of 12 cycles.
At age 62, patient developed grade 3 peripheral neuropathy after cumulative oxaliplatin exposure, requiring discontinuation of oxaliplatin; switched to 5-FU/leucovorin maintenance.
Surveillance CT at age 62 (3 months post-surgery) showed no evidence of recurrent disease; CEA was 4.2 ng/mL.
At age 62, CEA continued to trend down to 2.8 ng/mL and CA 19-9 normalized to 22 U/mL.
CT at age 63 (12 months post-surgery) revealed two new hepatic lesions in segments V and VI concerning for recurrence; CEA rose to 28 ng/mL.
At age 63, patient started second-line FOLFIRI (irinotecan 180 mg/m2, 5-FU, leucovorin) plus bevacizumab for recurrent hepatic metastases.
At age 63, patient developed grade 2 diarrhea on FOLFIRI, managed with loperamide and dose reduction of irinotecan to 150 mg/m2.
Restaging CT after 4 cycles of FOLFIRI-bevacizumab showed stable disease in hepatic lesions; CEA decreased to 18 ng/mL.
At age 63, patient continued FOLFIRI-bevacizumab with stable disease, performance status ECOG 1, and ongoing grade 1 neuropathy from prior oxaliplatin.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
Colonoscopic biopsy of sigmoid mass showed moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma (histology 8140/3), with tumor infiltrating through the muscularis propria.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
Next-generation sequencing of tumor tissue identified KRAS G12D mutation (c.35G>A), BRAF wild-type, PIK3CA wild-type, microsatellite stable (MSS) by IHC with intact MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, and PMS2 expression.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at age 61 demonstrated a sigmoid colon mass with multiple bilobar hepatic metastases (largest 4.2 cm in segment VII) and no pulmonary metastases; staging T3N2M1a.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 61, tumor board recommended palliative systemic therapy with FOLFOX plus bevacizumab given left-sided primary, KRAS mutant status, and liver-limited metastatic disease with potential for conversion to resectability.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

scenario_blurb: Stage IV colorectal cancer with KRAS G12D mutation. Left-sided and right-sided primary tumors. Treated with FOLFOX or FOLFIRI plus bevacizumab. Some with liver-limited metastases considered for hepatic resection. MSI/MMR testing performed.
